steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content: Divide both sides of $$3|x-7| = k$$ by 3: $$|x-7| = \frac{k}{3}$$

steps.title: Determine when the absolute value equation has exactly one solution
steps.content:
For any constant c, the equation |x - 7| = c has:
  • No solution when c < 0 (absolute value can’t be negative)
  • Exactly one solution when c = 0 (only x = 7 works)
  • Exactly two solutions when c > 0 (one on each side of 7)
So exactly one solution requires k/3 = 0.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-7$ only: k/3 = −7 means |x−7| = −7, which has no solutions at all. ✗
  • Choice B — $-7 \text{ or } 7$: k/3 = −7 gives no solutions; k/3 = 7 gives two solutions. Neither yields exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — $0$ only: k/3 = 0 means |x−7| = 0, so x = 7 is the unique solution. ✓
  • Choice D — $7$ only: k/3 = 7 means |x−7| = 7, giving x = 14 and x = 0 — two solutions, not one. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
